persona_label: Component 1 example: technical role (AI Integration Intern)
documented_expected_behaviour: Probe systems and architecture, technical depth, data and integrations, production versus prototype expectations, security and privacy, reliability and monitoring, debugging, testing, deployment, documentation, stakeholder translation, and acceptable equivalent technologies.
initial_manager_statement: We need an AI Integration Intern to help embed AI workflows into the Marketing team. They'll build small internal tools and prototypes for us.

turns.manager_message: We need an AI Integration Intern to help embed AI workflows into the Marketing team. They'll build small internal tools and prototypes for us.

next_question.question: Which Marketing AI workflows or use cases should the intern prioritize for their internal tools and prototypes?

turns.manager_message: The first project is a campaign-reporting tool that pulls data from our ad platforms and Google Sheets and summarizes it automatically.

next_question.question: Which ad platforms must the campaign-reporting tool connect to, and what should its automatic summary include?

turns.manager_message: It connects to Meta Ads and Google Ads, and the summary should flag campaigns that are overspending versus their weekly budget.

next_question.question: Should a campaign be flagged only after its total weekly spend exceeds budget, or when its projected end-of-week spend is expected to exceed budget?

turns.manager_message: Flag it once projected end-of-week spend is expected to exceed budget, not just after the fact.

next_question.question: Which specific AI workflow or use case should the intern prioritize first?

rejected_turns.note: A short single-fact reliability statement, not a compound sentence, was rejected four consecutive times (three via the runner, one via direct debug inspection): the model treats 'runs reliably without supervision' as both a must-have and an open question about what 'reliably' means, quoting the same short sentence for both. Broader evidence for the same systemic guard finding as docs/DECISIONS_AND_LESSONS.md, 2026-07-24 'Live persona runs: compound answers trip the must-have/ambiguity guard' -- this shows the trigger is not limited to compound sentences.
rejected_turns.manager_message: It needs to run reliably every day without supervision.

basic_info.title: AI Integration Intern
basic_info.initial_manager_statement: We need an AI Integration Intern to help embed AI workflows into the Marketing team. They'll build small internal tools and prototypes for us.

requirements.name: Build small internal AI tools and prototypes
requirements.description: Contribute by building small internal tools and prototypes that support embedding AI workflows into the Marketing team.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager states that the intern will build small internal tools and prototypes and help embed AI workflows into Marketing.
requirements.source_statement: We need an AI Integration Intern to help embed AI workflows into the Marketing team. They'll build small internal tools and prototypes for us.

requirements.name: Support AI workflow integration for Marketing
requirements.description: Help embed AI workflows into the Marketing team.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager explicitly identifies the Marketing team as the area where AI workflows are to be embedded.
requirements.source_statement: We need an AI Integration Intern to help embed AI workflows into the Marketing team. They'll build small internal tools and prototypes for us.

requirements.name: Campaign-reporting tool data retrieval
requirements.description: The first project involves a campaign-reporting tool that pulls data from ad platforms and Google Sheets.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager explicitly identifies the tool's data sources and retrieval function for the first project.
requirements.source_statement: The first project is a campaign-reporting tool that pulls data from our ad platforms and Google Sheets and summarizes it automatically.

requirements.name: Automated campaign-report summarization
requirements.description: The campaign-reporting tool summarizes retrieved data automatically.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager explicitly states that the tool will summarize the data automatically.
requirements.source_statement: The first project is a campaign-reporting tool that pulls data from our ad platforms and Google Sheets and summarizes it automatically.

requirements.name: Meta Ads connection
requirements.description: Connect the campaign-reporting tool to Meta Ads.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager identifies Meta Ads as a platform the tool connects to.
requirements.source_statement: It connects to Meta Ads and Google Ads, and the summary should flag campaigns that are overspending versus their weekly budget.

requirements.name: Google Ads connection
requirements.description: Connect the campaign-reporting tool to Google Ads.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager identifies Google Ads as a platform the tool connects to.
requirements.source_statement: It connects to Meta Ads and Google Ads, and the summary should flag campaigns that are overspending versus their weekly budget.

requirements.name: Weekly-budget overspend flagging
requirements.description: Generate summaries that flag campaigns overspending relative to their weekly budget.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager specifies the summary output should identify campaigns exceeding weekly budget.
requirements.source_statement: It connects to Meta Ads and Google Ads, and the summary should flag campaigns that are overspending versus their weekly budget.

requirements.name: Projected weekly budget overspend flagging
requirements.description: Flag campaigns when projected end-of-week spend is expected to exceed budget, rather than only after spend has exceeded the budget.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager specifies that flagging should be based on projected end-of-week spend exceeding budget.
requirements.source_statement: Flag it once projected end-of-week spend is expected to exceed budget, not just after the fact.

open_assumptions.statement: The internal tools and prototypes are intended to support the Marketing team's AI workflow integration.
open_assumptions.source_statement: We need an AI Integration Intern to help embed AI workflows into the Marketing team. They'll build small internal tools and prototypes for us.

open_assumptions.statement: The campaign-reporting tool is likely one of the small internal AI tools or prototypes already captured for the role.
open_assumptions.source_statement: The first project is a campaign-reporting tool that pulls data from our ad platforms and Google Sheets and summarizes it automatically.

open_ambiguities.description: The specific AI workflows to be embedded, the intended use cases, and the scope of the tools and prototypes are not stated.
open_ambiguities.source_statement: help embed AI workflows into the Marketing team. They'll build small internal tools and prototypes for us.
open_ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: These details are needed to define the work the intern would perform and the relevant technical capabilities.

open_ambiguities.description: The specific ad platforms to be connected, the Google Sheets access method or data structure, and the expected format and audience for the automatic summaries need confirmation.
open_ambiguities.source_statement: The first project is a campaign-reporting tool that pulls data from our ad platforms and Google Sheets and summarizes it automatically.
open_ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: These details determine the integrations and the functional scope of the project.

open_ambiguities.description: The definition of a campaign being "overspending versus their weekly budget" needs confirmation, including whether it means total spend has exceeded the weekly budget or projected weekly spend is expected to exceed it.
open_ambiguities.source_statement: the summary should flag campaigns that are overspending versus their weekly budget.
open_ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: The calculation and flagging logic cannot be determined from the statement alone.

blockers: At least one success outcome is required.
At least one responsibility is required.
At least one justified must-have requirement is required.
At least one assessment method is required.
A human decision owner is required.
